title: Fix transmission of signals by Test Automation Executor
body:
The order of signals obtained by pMsg->GetSignalList( signalList ); is
determined by keys of the map. In this case it is ISignal* and
having items sorted on the address of a pointer makes no sense.
Moreover on every invocation of GetSignalList() we get another map of
pointers so perhaps there is a memory leak as well.

The order of signals in ouSendData.m_odSignalDataList is determined by the signals
present in the testsetup xml file and we cannot make any assumptions on this.

So the signals in the signalList (containing startaddresses, endianess etc)
must be matched to the ouSendData.m_odSignalDataList on signalName.

The GetProperties (DBManager.dll ?) function previously used in un64GetBitMask
did not initialize m_ouEndianess and this results in unpredictable results.
For CAN this was the last place in BUSMASTER m_ouEndianess was used.

title: Fix Transmission of messages by Test Automation Executor
body:

Remove unneeded typecasting in IxxatCanChannel.cpp.

This will fix issue #1098

title: Added support for CAN Saint hardware
body:
As a summer project I have provided support for the Saint hardware ([https://www.dgtech.com/saint2/](url)) within Busmaster. Along with providing support for the existing tools within Busmaster, I also created a tool specific to Saint that provides functionality implemented within other Saint software.

The Saint tool provides the following features: 
- Message transmission with the support of .msg files
- Message filtering based on .flt files
- Group file viewing and editing
- Periodic message transmission based on .per files
- Message emulation with .emu file support

title: Polished LDFEditor and LDFViewer
body:
I did some housekeeping/gardening around the two UI modules written entirely in Qt to move ahead to the multi platform approach goals mentioned in the #45 
This includes:
- Adding pro (Qt project) files 
- Fixes around the case sensitivity
- Some tweaks around the MS only types
- Some code polishing like utilizing the connectsSlotsByName and so on.

I have not been able to test it because the DataModule library is not yet buildable on Linux.

title: First modifications to #13
body:
-First changes to allow association of different databases per channel -
#13

-Identification of messages using its name, not the CAN-ID which is not
unique if more than one channel is used - maybe solves #669
-Uniqueness in message-names is reached by
channelnumber::framename-format, e. g. CAN1::Dummyframe

Still some work to do, to reach perfect solution of #13, but first steps
are done.

title: Channel-Selection for Test Automation
body:
I implemented a channel-selection for the Test Automation according to Issue #244.
Due to VerifyResponse does currently not work (#697) the channel-selection is only available for Send and Verify.
Additionally the line-coloring-error (#698) is solved.
